reference.title: Candy Apples
reference.shortdesc: Candy apples are whole apples coated in a hardened sugar or candy shell and are commonly associated with fairs, festivals, and Halloween.

section.title: Overview
section.p: Candy apples are typically made by dipping apples into a hot sugar mixture that hardens into a glossy coating as it cools. They are often served on sticks to make them easier to handle and eat.

section.title: Common Coatings
section.p: Candy apples may be coated with a variety of sweet mixtures, including:

section.title: Typical Occasions
section.p: Candy apples are most often prepared and sold during autumn events, such as Halloween celebrations, county fairs, and seasonal festivals.
